title: Stelis rictoria
intro: Stelis rictoria, also known as Queen's Wreath, is a flowering plant species in the orchid family that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis rictoria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis rictoria is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat respiratory infections, sore throats, and other conditions, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
